name: create-survey-solicitation
narrative: Maya has just finished designing her Round 6 study — the two-arm Attakar-versus-Gura household survey that her Vitamin-A programme will use to check whether its visits are actually changing coverage. She has the plan; what she does not yet have is the independent survey firm to run it. So from the plan she just built, Maya clicks "Create solicitation" and lets the plan itself become the call for a firm. On the create form the R6 plan is already waiting as the coverage area, so Maya only sets the survey scope and publishes the open call. Across the country, Amina — who runs an independent survey firm — opens the public solicitation, reads the coverage on offer, ticks the Attakar-and-Gura area as the work her enumerators can staff, and submits her firm's application. Back in her workspace, Maya opens the responses and sees Amina's firm tied to the exact coverage area she designed — the loop from a study plan to a real applicant closed. Awarding that firm and standing up the live Connect opportunity her enumerators would actually run is the step Maya is reaching for next; today she has recruited and can compare applicants against the real coverage.

amina.name: Amina Okafor
amina.role: Director, independent survey firm (Health Bridge Nigeria)
amina.intro: Amina runs an independent household-survey firm and is looking for survey work she can staff; she answers public solicitations with the coverage her enumerators can commit to.

maya.name: Maya
maya.intro: Maya designed the Round 6 two-arm Attakar-versus-Gura study for her Vitamin-A programme and now needs to recruit the independent firm that will run the household survey on it.

slides.title: Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
slides.narration: Maya stands in her programme portfolio, where the Round 6 study she just designed — Attakar as the intervention arm, Gura as the comparison — sits as a study group; instead of hunting for a survey firm in a separate tool, she clicks "Create solicitation" right on that R6 group card to turn it into an open call.

slides.title: Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
slides.narration: On the create form Maya sees her R6 plan already sitting in a "Coverage areas (from micro-plan)" panel — the title and scope pre-filled from it — so she only writes the survey scope and publishes the call rather than re-describing the area she just designed.

slides.title: Amina reads the open call and ticks the area she can staff
slides.narration: Amina opens the public solicitation, reads that the work is the Attakar-and-Gura household survey, and on the application form ticks that exact coverage area as the one her enumerators can staff before submitting her firm's application.

slides.title: Maya sees the firm tied to the coverage she designed
slides.narration: Back in her workspace Maya opens the responses and reads, on the one application, the exact coverage area she designed — R6 Attakar x Gura — so she is comparing a real firm against the real survey, the loop from study plan to applicant closed.

slides.title: Maya looks ahead to standing up the real opportunity
slides.narration: With a firm tied to her coverage, Maya looks at the Award action and knows the next step she is reaching for — turning an award into the live Connect opportunity her enumerators would actually run — is the work ahead, not something today's call has done for her.
